title: Chemical Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run process-simulation sweeps and draft mass/energy balance reports, but plant safety analysis, scale-up judgment, and regulatory sign-off remain engineer-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: Process simulation software (Aspen, gPROMS) has credible AI-assisted optimization add-ons, but they are not yet the industry default.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in large petrochemical and pharma manufacturers; most mid-size process plants still run traditional simulation workflows.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run parameter sweeps across a process model and flag deviations, but physical plant commissioning and hazard review need an on-site engineer.

tasksAtRisk: Process simulation parameter sweeps and optimization runs
Mass and energy balance report drafting
tasksGrowing: Plant safety and hazard analysis (HAZOP) leadership
Scale-up judgment from lab to production
Regulatory compliance sign-off for new processes
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2041, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Simulation sweeps and balance calculations increasingly run themselves, but hazard analysis and scale-up decisions still ride on an engineer's judgment. Chemical engineers who own plant safety leadership hold the highest-value ground.
